text: "title": "Passengers with reduced mobility and special needs "url": "https://help.vueling.com/hc/en-gb/articles/19798820075537-Passengers-with-reduced-mobility-and-special-needs "locale": "en-gb "body": "If you have a disability of any kind or your mobility is reduced, we give you all the information you need to travel.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: Passengers travelling in a wheelchair Passengers with limbs in plaster casts Passengers with mobility problems, impaired sight and/or hearing Passengers with intellectual disability Passengers who are not self-sufficient (e.g. It is important to think about all aspects of the trip, including arriving at the airport, which can be a bit confusing and stressful. In these cases it is mandatory to always travel with a companion. Please bear in mind that the airport assistance service is only available for minors if they are accompanied . If you are travelling with children with autism , you can consult additional information that may come in handy on the day of your trip . How to request assistance In order to guarantee the quality of the service provided, we recommend to request assistance at least 48 hours before the departure of the flight , since the number of people requiring special assistance who can travel on a flight is limited. If you don't give advance notice, you may not be provided with the assistance you need and your travel plans may be affected.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: If you make your purchase through our website , you can let us know during the purchasing process and we will request the assistance you need at the airports. You just need to state your type of need and whether you are bringing an assistance dog . If you did not inform us of your needs during the purchase process or made the purchase through our app , you can do so through our website : REQUEST ASSISTANCE The request for assistance service can also be made through these phone numbers, which are exclusively dedicated to supporting people with special needs: Spain 931222230 France +33185148077 Italy +390694802424 UK +442045710521 Rest of the world +34931222251 When is it necessary to be accompanied?

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: All passengers must be self-sufficient in the following cases: Understanding safety instructions Fastening and unfastening their seat belts Finding the life jacket and putting it on Leaving their seats and going to an emergency exit Putting on their oxygen masks Using the toilet independently (no accessible toilet) If they are not able to do these things, for their own safety and that of other passengers, they must travel with a non-disabled adult companion over 18 years of age who can provide them with the necessary assistance. Otherwise, they will be denied boarding. Booking, seat assignment and check-in Passengers with special needs, as well as one companion, are entitled to a free seat adapted to their needs. They will be assigned during the online check-in process (7 days before departure) or by contacting us through these dedicated phone numbers: Spain 931222230 France +33185148077 Italy +390694802424 UK +442045710521 Rest of the world +34931222251 If necessary, this can also be requested from the crew on the day of the flight.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: To ensure suitable placement and make it easier to travel together, it is recommended to check in as early as possible within the available time frame. According to our internal security procedures, the total number of unaccompanied or accompanied customers with special needs and their companions must not exceed 10% of the aircraft's capacity. These maximums may be exceeded in the case of groups that have previously requested it for specific dates or flights, on an exceptional basis. The group request must be made as far in advance as possible via our Customer Service Centre . For safety reasons, passengers with disabilities or reduced mobility are normally allocated window seats. If this is unsuitable, please contact our Customer Services Centre for reassignment. Seats in the first/last rows and at or near emergency exits cannot be occupied. In addition, under specific circumstances, additional seats must be booked : Passengers with plaster casts from the waist up : they will only need to reserve one seat. Passengers with plaster casts from the waist down (whole leg): they will need to book three seats to travel.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: Passengers with plaster cast only on lower leg: they must book two seats to travel. Hand luggage and medical equipment Passengers who book the airport assistance service, whichever fare they choose, are allowed to carry one piece of luggage on board (max. 10 kg and 55x40x20 cm), at no extra cost, as well as one underseat cabin bag (max. 40x20x30 cm). We also offer you the possibility of checking in this luggage for free if you prefer. As well as hand luggage, other items may be carried on board such as crutches, walking stick, umbrella, walking frame and apnoea machines . You can also carry medicine or a diabetes kit in your underseat cabin bag or cabin bag (medical certificate required). On the day of your flight Once you've requested assistance, you should go to the meeting point at the airport at least 2.5 hours before scheduled departure . Departure and arrival services are very different depending on the destination of your flight and are provided by the airport, we recommend that you check the airport website to check information about the facilities and services offered.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: You can also go directly to the Vueling check-in desks and we'll let the assistance service know you're here. Wheelchair check-in If you are travelling with a wheelchair or mobility device, we have prepared an article containing all the information you need to know. Boarding, on board the plane and disembarkation Customers with reduced mobility are the first to board to ensure that they can be seated on the plane as comfortably as possible. Depending on the assistance requested, the airport assistance service will accompany you to the boarding gate, to the aircraft door or to your seat. As part of our commitment to accessibility and to continuously improving our customers’ experience, at Vueling we now have onboard wheelchairs across our entire fleet to assist passengers with reduced mobility when moving around the cabin and to ensure a more comfortable and inclusive journey. During the flight, our crew will be happy to assist you in any way they can. Safety leaflets in braille are available in all our aircraft upon request. Once the plane lands, assisted customers will be the last to disembark and will be escorted to the location they indicate to the ground staff.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: Traveling with an assistance dog If you are travelling with an assistance dog, you can consult all the relevant information . Damage or loss of mobility equipment If your mobility equipment is delayed, damaged, or lost upon arrival at your destination, you must report it to the Lost and Found office before leaving the arrivals terminal. Check for more information on luggage incidents . "created_at": "2023-11-01T11:23:57Z "title": "Wheelchair Check-in "url": "https://help.vueling.com/hc/en-gb/articles/30891224305425-Wheelchair-Check-in "locale": "en-gb "body": "If you are travelling with a wheelchair or mobility aid, remember that you can check in up to two mobility devices free of charge (for example, a wheelchair and a walker, etc.). Documentation If your wheelchair or mobility aid has a battery, make sure to check the restrictions on transporting battery-powered wheelchairs and the transport requirements depending on the type of battery. Remember that you can consult the details of your device, such as battery type, dimensions, weight and how to disconnect the battery, in the manufacturer’s instructions.

section: Who can request assistance at the airport?
text: "body": "For safety reasons, if you are travelling with a wheelchair or mobility aid with a battery, the device must be checked to ensure it meets all requirements before being accepted on board. This verification will be carried out at the airport, so it is recommended to bring all the necessary documentation to allow the following checks: Type of device, brand and model Weight (in kg) and dimensions of the device: length, height and width Type of battery (and watt-hours for lithium batteries) Information on how to disable circuits and isolate the battery to prevent it from being switched on accidentally during the flight If the wheelchair or mobility aid does not meet the required conditions, its transport will have to be refused. Battery type Description Non-spillable Dry battery (including integrated battery) Gel battery Wet (sealed) battery • The battery terminals and other electrical circuits must be isolated to prevent the device from being accidentally switched on. • The battery must be securely attached to the device or removed according to the manufacturer's instructions (if designed to do so). • You can carry a maximum of one spare battery , with no power limitation.

"url": "https://help.vueling.com/hc/en-gb/articles/30894934000401-Other-Special-Needs "locale": "en-gb "body": "For more information related to other types of special needs, please see our informative articles on: Larger passengers and travelling with an extra seat Flying while pregnant Flying with children and infants Unaccompanied minors Travelling with pets Travelling with medication "created_at": "2024-12-12T12:05:28Z "title": "Assistance or Service Animal / Guide Dog "url": "https://help.vueling.com/hc/en-gb/articles/19798812263697-Assistance-or-Service-Animal-Guide-Dog "locale": "en-gb "body": "Assistance or service dogs are those that have been individually trained to help people with a special need . Emotional support animals are not globally recognized as a service animal. If you need to travel with an assistance dog, you must meet the following general requirements : Indicate it during the booking process in the Passenger details section by selecting the Special needs passengers option.

You can also notify us at least 48 hours in advance of the flight departure through our Customer Service Centre, via our virtual assistant or by calling the exclusive assistance service: Spain 931222230 France +33185148077 Italy +390694802424 UK +442045710521 Rest of the world +34931222251 The maximum number of assistance dogs allowed on board is 2, in accordance with our internal safety procedures. This limit may only be exceeded in exceptional cases, so it is essential that the request is made as early as possible in order to manage it properly. If the notification is made less than 48 hours in advance, we will do our best to confirm that there is no issue with flying with your assistance dog, although ideally we recommend notifying us in advance. To validate the required documentation before the flight, it is also important to contact our Customer Service Centre via our virtual assistant or by calling the exclusive assistance service. Present an official physical certificate qualifying it as an assistance dog or guide dog . Bear in mind that a doctor’s note is not enough to certify that you need to travel with an assistance animal.

If the dog does not have the above mentioned official certificate, it will not be admitted on board. The training of the assistance dog must have been certified by an accredited organization , such as Assistance Dogs International (ADI), International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF), Assistance Dogs Assessment Association (ADAA), or the Spanish National Organization for the Blind (ONCE). The dog must be at least 3 months old and be duly identified through a tattoo or microchip. Carry with you the dog's immunization record . The provided documents must certify that the dog has been vaccinated for rabies at least 21 days before the flight. For Sweden, Ireland and United Kingdom it is also required a tapeworm treatment. Vet must include the following details: Name and manufacturer of the product, date and time they treated your dog, stamp and signature. For international flights, a passport for the animal will also be required (ask your veterinarian how to request it). If you are traveling outside the European Union, check the travel requirements with the consulate of the destination country.

Flights from/to the UK In addition to the general requirements, you must comply with the requirements established by the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (DEFRA), which include: Notifying the airline at least 48 hours before the flight’s departure (during the booking process or through the Customer Service Center) that you are travelling with an assistance dog. Training certification : your assistance dog must be trained by an accredited organization member of Assistance Dogs International (ADI), the International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF), or the Assistance Dogs Assessment Association (ADAA). If trained elsewhere, you must provide proof that the training meets or exceeds the standards set by these organizations. Written declaration : a statement from you as the dog's owner or handler, detailing the specific tasks your dog performs to assist with your disability. Assistance Dog ID card : It is recommended to help facilitate the verification process at the airport. Flights to/from Ireland The following will be required, in addition to the general requirements: EU or Schengen countries: tapeworm treatment.

Rest of countries: Tapeworm treatment, blood test showing the treatment was effective, and send an email to petmove@agriculture.gov.ie informing about your pet. If you are only travelling to Ireland , as well as the documents listed above, you will need: For passengers arriving from countries outside the EU or the Schengen area, notice must be made to the Ministry of Agriculture, Food and the Marine using the Pet Import Portal page , at least 24 hours before the scheduled arrival time.

For acceptance, the dog must be clean, well-behaved, and under the passenger's control at all times. The dog must remain on the floor, under the seat, and be secured with a safety harness before and during takeoff and landing. For safety reasons, dogs cannot block any aisles or other areas used for emergency evacuations. You must bring the necessary care items for the dog, such as a water bowl, blanket, absorbent pads, sanitary bags, and others. "created_at": "2023-11-01T11:24:07Z "title": "Accessible communication with Vueling "url": "https://help.vueling.com/hc/en-gb/articles/37597205080849-Accessible-communication-with-Vueling "locale": "en-gb "body": "At Vueling, we are committed to accessibility and inclusivity. That’s why we offer the Hearlo app . Hearlo is a free app that facilitates communication between our staff and people with hearing impairments, language barriers, or speech disorders.

Hearlo helps to improve the flow of communication , without the need for interpreters. It also creates a more accessible and autonomous airport experience. To do so, it offers the following options: Messaging feature : Based on written messages, it generates audio that can also be translated into the language you need. The app includes up to 40 languages. Hearlo Cards: It generates an audio message from a series of pictograms linked to the activities that take place at the airport: check-in, boarding, etc. Shortcuts: It generates an audio message from a series of phrases linked to activities that take place at the airport: check-in, boarding, etc. Phrases: It enables you to build complex sentences and generate audio from them. It combines text and pictograms. Tours: It describes all the steps to follow from when you arrive the airport, including check-in, going through security and boarding, as well as the flight and getting off the plane. The app can be used easily at any time during your trip, both at the airport and on board the plane, and without an internet connection . But remember to download it first!

It's very simple. Just follow these steps: Scan the QR code below to download the app. If you can’t scan the QR code, download it using this link . Choose the communication method that best suits your needs.

"url": "https://help.vueling.com/hc/en-gb/articles/19798834819345-Extra-Seat "locale": "en-gb "body": "You can book the Extra Seat service if you wish to enjoy more space and privacy, if you're travelling with a musical instrument or have a plaster cast, for instance. The Extra Seat will always be adjoining the seat allocated to you for the flight and you can book it for any area of the plane (except emergency exits). Larger passengers can also request a double space to fly by booking an extra seat. If you have a plaster cast and cannot bend your knee, in addition to the seat in which you will be travelling, you will have to book two adjoining seats through Customer Services . Remember: stretchers are not allowed on board.
